reasoning: The probability of OpenAI formally declaring they have created Artificial General Intelligence by June 30, 2025, is extremely low. There are only 8 days left until the resolution date. The last time the prediction market traded on this event (June 12), the probability was at 1%. My review of news and discussions throughout May and June 2025 shows no new rumors, leaks, or impending milestones scheduled before the end of the month. Furthermore, OpenAI is heavily disincentivized from making an official AGI declaration due to clauses in their contract with Microsoft, which could radically alter their commercial arrangements. While Sam Altman and OpenAI frequently tease AGI and discuss 2025 as the year "agents join the workforce," they have carefully avoided outright declaring that they have *achieved* AGI, even after releasing advanced reasoning models like o3. In the absence of an imminent, heavily foreshadowed media event to announce humanity's biggest technological leap, this will almost certainly not happen in the next 8 days.
